audit_items.proof_trace: Every error-exit in do_add_delta (NON_NUMERIC, DELTA_ITEM_CAS_MISMATCH, EOM, DELTA_ITEM_NOT_FOUND) calls do_item_remove(it) before returning; the new_it allocation is released on failure (do_item_remove+return EOM) and on success (do_item_remove(new_it)); the it_ret output pointer is set only on the OK path, so callers in proto_text.c (process_arithmetic_command, process_marithmetic_command) never receive a dangling reference on error. The process_marithmetic_command error label (L1989-1994) performs if(it!=NULL) do_item_remove(it) covering all goto-error paths including mid-loop jumps, and the vivify item_alloc path is also covered by do_item_remove at L1959 on the normal exit.
